title: Data Insights Analyst
description: I’m partnering with a well\-established Irish organisation to hire a Data Insights Analyst to join their Dublin team. This is a hybrid working model which will require you to be onsite in the Dublin office 2 days per week. This is an excellent opportunity for someone with strong data analysis experience who enjoys working in a commercially focused environment, using data to drive real business decisions. You’ll play a key role in understanding customer behaviour, content performance and subscription trends, helping the business improve engagement, conversion and overall revenue performance. **What you’ll be doing:** * Analyzing customer behaviour, content performance and subscription trends * Identifying opportunities across acquisition, engagement, retention and churn * Building and maintaining dashboards and automated reporting tools * Managing tracking across platforms including event and conversion tracking * Supporting commercial teams with insights to drive revenue opportunities * Working closely with marketing, editorial, product and finance teams * Defining and refining key metrics and KPIs across the business **Experience we are looking for:** * 3\+ years’ experience in data analysis * Strong understanding of digital metrics including engagement, retention, funnels, subscriptions and customer segmentation * Experience building dashboards and presenting insights in a clear, actionable way * Hands\-on experience with tracking, reporting and data analysis tools * Strong communication skills with the ability to explain data to non\-technical stakeholders * Comfortable working cross\-functionally with multiple teams across the business * Experience with tools such as GA4, Google Tag Manager, Power BI, Excel, HubSpot, Microsoft Clarity, SEMrush or similar would be an advantage This is a hands\-on role where you’ll own the data journey end\-to\-end, from how it’s captured through to how it’s used to inform decision\-making, so you’ll need to be comfortable working with both the technical and commercial side of data. Due to the high volume of applications we receive, only suited candidates will be contacted.

title: Deal Advisory Trainee
description: **We are Grant Thornton. We go beyond business as usual, so you can too.** Grant Thornton Ireland (GT) has over 2,500 people in 10 offices across Ireland, The Isle of Man, Gibraltar, and Bermuda, with a presence in over 130 countries around the world, and a global network of over 62,000 people At GT, we work as trusted advisors, bringing local knowledge and national expertise, with a global presence, to help businesses succeed – wherever they are located. We make business more personal by investing in building relationships and empowering our clients to make the right decisions for their organisation now and for the future. Whether that is working with the public sector to build thriving communities, with regulators and financial institutions to build trust, or with a diverse range of businesses to help them achieve their goals, Grant Thornton Ireland work hard to support clients to act on the issues that matter. At GT Ireland we don’t just predict your future, we build it. **A Career at GT** Looking for a more fulfilling role in professional services? One where fresh thinking, collaboration and diversity are valued? At Grant Thornton we do things differently. **What does this mean for you?** A career in a more inclusive working environment, a more collaborative work culture, a more supported, flexible working role, more possibilities to grow and more opportunities to help shape the future for your clients. We respect and value your experience. And we want you to bring your authentic self to work and be at your best. It is how it should be. **Grow with us** At Grant Thornton, we care about our people and work hard to make you feel valued. If you are looking to deepen and develop your skills, knowledge, and experience throughout your career, then that is what you will get, and more. Due to our continued growth, we have an exciting opportunity available within our Deal Advisory department. The ideal candidate will be a recent graduate, keen to undertake professional accountancy exams, who possesses a high degree of commercial awareness and attention to detail. This role provides strong candidates with the opportunity to become a key part of a rapidly growing area of the business and offers highly attractive career development and progression opportunities. The Deal Advisory department in Grant Thornton has extensive experience in all aspects of corporate finance from mergers and acquisitions, transaction advisory services, valuations and raising capital and business planning. Through your training you will gain the professional accountancy qualification while undertaking a variety of work, including: * Conducting detailed financial reviews and due diligence investigations * Preparing business plans * Assisting with sale and purchase mandates, MBO and MBI assignments * Assisting with the preparation of valuation reports * Financial modelling * Liaising with clients, financial institutions and solicitors * Assisting in preparation of presentations and marketing initiatives Having the opportunity to work on short term and long\-term assignments on clients’ premises * **If you are a recent graduate in Finance, Corporate Finance, Economics and available to start immediately, please get in touch for more information or apply via the link!** \#LI\-KS1

title: Design Engineer
description: **About Tate** At Tate we are passionate about everything we do. As an independent brand operating within Kingspan Group, a global plc, Tate has been recognised worldwide as an industry leader in the development and manufacture of data centre infrastructure solutions and commercial office raised access floors for over 60 years. With revenues of over $500m and growing, Tate plays a pivotal role in offering expertise in cutting edge design engineering, working collaboratively with clients as a trusted partner. As a market leader within our industry Tate continues to grow and expand, operating 12 manufacturing sites across the US, Europe, and Australia. We are excited about our fresh, dynamic, and inclusive team of experts working on new innovations and forward\-thinking designs. We continue to invest heavily in the best available manufacturing tools and equipment needed to adhere to Tate's world class standards. In keeping with our Planet Passionate sustainability strategy, our mission is to accelerate a net zero emissions future built environment with the wellbeing of people and planet at its heart. **About the Role** The Design Engineer will be responsible for new product creation, prototype design and development, costing, and qualification processes for products primarily for the data centre industry but also may be required to assist in design for emerging industries including indoor agriculture, corrections and other industries with a focus on factory fabrications. The Design Engineer will work closely with the business development team to identify new opportunities, develop conceptual designs and drawings to be presented to potential customers. They will also work very closely with the Manufacturing team to ensure designs are optimal, practical, energy and cost efficient. **Key Responsibilities** Perform Value Design Engineering including detailed 3D layouts, schematic design, and cost Estimation for mechanical plant to go into the Data Centre Industry. Prepare and present detailed quotations, BOM's and specifications to Kingspan's customers direct and for tender submissions. liaise with third party suppliers in requesting quotations. Liaise and assist the internal project team/s with variation costs and cost data for live projects. Monitor and maintain company KPI's. Provide information to other Kingspan commercial Teams for preparation of yearly budgets and project review meetings. Maintaining and adhering to the ISO9001, ISO14001 and other relevant standards. Be a leading resource for our multilevel data centre customers, End Users, Developers, Architects \& Engineers, Main Contractors and Sub\-contractors. Design packages which incorporate a Group\-wide product offering for data centres, maximising all of Kingspan's offerings across different divisions. Help share the strategy for Kingspan Tate for the future. Embed the Group's message of Planet Passionate in all dialogue with customers and data centre stakeholders. Be able to handle highly confidential, sensitive and non\-routine information to help support a rapidly transforming organisation. Support continual process improvement initiatives with accurate \& meaningful data to drive the functions enhancements. **Qualifications, Experience \& Skills** **Education:** HNC / Degree / Post Graduate Degree Qualifications in Building Services Engineering. **Experience:** 3\-5 years' experience with CAD design, Estimating in a manufacturing environment. Exposure to Data Centres or Critical environments is highly desirable. **Skills** * Good working knowledge of manufacturing processes, materials, and quality control protocols. * Experience developing new products and taking them from concept to prototype to production. * Strong communication and teamwork skills. * Excellent time management and organisational skills, with the ability to set objectives and prioritise workloads for yourself. * Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment and interface with sales, customer service, purchasing, manufacturing, engineering, quality and marketing.

title: Digital Engineering and BIM Roles
description: At Mace, our purpose is to redefine the boundaries of ambition. We believe in creating places that are responsible, bringing transformative impact to our people, communities and societies across the globe. To learn more about our purpose, culture, and priorities, visit our strategy site. Within our consult business we harness our unique combination of leading\-edge practical expertise and project delivery to unlock the potential in every project. **The project:** The project is a generational project providing Dublin and Ireland with a much\-needed world class Metro system to support the fast\-growing population. The project is an 18\.8km fully autonomous Metro system, consisting of 16 stations, 2 significant tunnelled section and a number of significant structures along the alignment. The project will integrate existing transportation systems through the city and surrounding areas, including the Dublin airport, Irish Rail and the LUAS. The successful candidate must lead by example and serve as a role model ensuring the highest standards are adopted across the project. Our values shape the way we consult, and define the people we want to join us on our journey: **Safety First \- Going Home Safe And Well** * You will be an advocate of Mace's value of safety first, and be accountable for leading and maintaining exceptional safety, quality, cost, programme, sustainability and project compliance standards. * You will possess technical expertise in local health and safety rules and regulations relevant to project portfolio. * You will champion a diverse and inclusive working environment and understand the importance of the wellbeing of the people you manage. **Client Focus – Deliver On Our Promise** * BIM is the keystone of modern delivery of high\-end infrastructure projects. It creates efficiencies and provides information that would otherwise cause substantial delays to the projects we work on in Mace. * BIM and digital engineering combined coupled with Delivery are here to drive forward major infrastructure projects across Rail, Water and Energy. We require a number of BIM individuals across a number of grades from Junior to Experienced Senior Candidates to work on Rail projects in Dublin. **You’ll Need To Have** * Autodesk desktop products. * Validation and verification tools. * Visualisation tools. * Databases. * Content management library systems. * Bentley product suite (ProjectWise, AssetWise, iTwin). * Understanding of ISO19650, BS1192, common data environment (CDE), IFC, COBie, and classification (Uniclass), international cost management standard (ICMS). * Rail/Metro experience (including traffic management, handover and phasing drawings). * 5\+ years' experience in delivering BIM projects according to international standards. **You’ll Also Have** * Certification in BIM management, experience with GIS. * Adobe products. Mace is an inclusive employer and welcomes interest from a diverse range of candidates. Even if you feel you do not fulfil all of the criteria, please apply as you may still be the best candidate for this role or another role within our organisation. We are also open to discussing part time, flexible, and hybrid working options if suitable within the role. Please remember the Employee Referral Scheme and if there are any talented people you can recommend, just click the button and use the ‘Refer a friend’ feature found within the relevant job. \-site

title: Digital SCM - Spark Foundry
description: #### **The Role** **OVERVIEW** This role assumes responsibility for managing the integration of digital aspects of the media planning activity and providing digital strategy planning insights for multiple client accounts within their client team. It requires management of processes, excellent attention to detail and time management skills to ensure best in class delivery on behalf of the media team for clients. This role supports the Digital Client Director in fostering strong relationships with clients. The role requires an ambitious, creative and innovative person who prides themselves on building strong and trustworthy client relationships. Digital Senior Client Managers require strong collaboration and communication with the wider team, particularly Social, Search and Programmatic teams in Investment. The ideal candidate will enjoy working in a fast\-paced environment with a drive to succeed and can multi\-task in a deadline driven atmosphere. **Responsibilities** This role will have the responsibility of developing the digital media rationale for their clients which, in turn, must be aligned to the overall strategic direction of the client. This role will also be the key day to day client contact and will ensure that all media decisions are made using a robust process powered by the appropriate media planning tools. Central to this role is a clear understanding of digital media planning principles, an ability to articulate the strengths and weaknesses of each media channel and the role that each will play in delivering on the overall strategy. This role will also establish the KPIs for the media plan and will ensure that all important information is clearly shown on the plan for client approval. The Digital Senior Client Manager will also manage the implementation and delivery of the digital media plan and will perform the required Quality Control to ensure that the plan produced matches the media rationale and the objectives set. Success will be defined at the yearend RADAR client service review in improvement in media planning and the impact of media ideas on the client’s business. **AGENCY PERFORMANCE** * Understanding of the key business challenge for their clients as well as the digital media strategy to help address these challenges * Responsibility for delivery of the digital media rationale and the digital media plan to align with the overall media strategy * Participate in planning sessions and work with the Digital Client Director to deliver an integrated media response to the client briefs * Input into Macro channel budget setting as well as establishing the media KPIs for briefing to Investment teams * Development of digital media brief and the coordination of internal briefings across Core Practices and Central Teams * Day to day client service and working closely with the Digital Client Manager to deliver to deadlines * Manage the quality control of the media plan to ensure that it matches the brief and the media rationale and that all relevant KPIs, target audiences and objectives are clearly outlined on the plans * Ensure any audit recommendations that have been agreed by Business Director and Client Director with auditor are implemented on all media plans, including working with Channel Planning and Investment teams to manage * Working with Digital Client Managers to ensure plans are audit ready and KPI document is up to date for each audit * Adopt and embrace an accuracy culture by having and supporting excellent attention to detail * Manage the intra\-campaign reporting and optimisation with the specialist teams * Work with the client to change plans / budget allocation where necessary * Liaise with client to obtain plan approval * Engage with media owners \& working with them to deliver partnerships in an efficient and collaborative manner * Responsible for digital activity at audit **COMMERCIAL PERFORMANCE** * Demonstrate a strong comprehension of the client’s business and their KPIs * Ensure all projects are delivered on time and on budget * Manage media budgets across all plans and Practice areas * Have a clear understanding of the scope of the client contracts including the key performance metrics * Ensure all billing is 100% accurate and on time for clients, and particularly on new specialised service projects * Work with the Digital Client Manager, Finance and Digital Client Director to monitor and manage aged debt, ensuring clients pay in a timely fashion * Ensure timesheets are updated weekly and that they accurately reflect the time spent on our clients business * Work with Digital Client Director to monitor profitability and revenue targets for your clients * Garner insight needed for the development of the media strategy **TALENT MANAGEMENT** * Commit to the internal Core Learning programme(s) and regularly attend training for your development * Collaborate closely with the Digital Client Director to ensure your personal development requirements are met * Ensure a strong comprehension and commitment to your career development plan in line with your 360 appraisal, quarterly catch up’s and goals * Assist in the management and development of junior team members while adhering to the performance management process in place * Uphold Core’s values in words and actions * Proactively build relationships within the wider company * Actively participate in knowledge sharing across the company and within your team * Contribute to a hardworking and fun team environment #### **Your profile** **EXPERIENCE** * Previous digital media experience in a similar role, with a proven track record in media planning and buying * Previous experience working with digital media tools * Knowledge of the broader digital disciplines * Excellent working knowledge of Microsoft Office, Excel and PowerPoint * Experience project managing large scale, complex media campaigns is essential * High level of business and financial acumen * Possess good presentation skills – assist in presenting to clients **Person Specification** * Be a confident communicator * Excellent interpersonal skills – ability to build strong relationships with clients * Display the ability to think creatively and innovatively * Very knowledgeable on all media – ideally an expert on at least one medium * Possess good presentation skills – assist in presenting insights toclients * Good listener \- ability to understand client’s objectives * Excellent attention to detail * Understands the importance of upskilling * Can think on their feet to make the right decision quickly * Highly organised individual that can manage multiple client projects and the overall delivery of the work * Understands and appreciates the benefit of processes * Be responsive to requests * Know when to escalate and involve other people #### **Why us?** * Flexible and hybrid working model, within the Republic of Ireland * 24/7 access to professional development through LinkedIn Learning along with career progression and mentoring * The opportunity to work across a huge range of clients within so many different categories from sport, retail, food and drink, to alcohol, insurance, banking and so much more * 21 days annual leave plus a day off on your birthday, company closure days at Christmas and early finish on bank holiday Fridays * A company culture that promotes work\-life balance * Family\-focused benefits including paid maternity and paternity leave as well as maternity phase back program, fertility treatment supports and paid pregnancy loss leave * Access to mental health and well\-being programs including paid health insurance for you and your dependents under 18 * Professional financial advice; employer contributory pension and life assurance * Make a difference by joining our D\&I, CSR, Green, Social or Great Place to Work committees; you’ll get extra days leave for your efforts. * And lots more … #### **Salary Range** 48,000\-56,000 DOE #### **About us** Core is Ireland's largest marketing communications company with over 400 people working across the key areas of marketing communications \- research, data science, strategy, planning, creative development and implementation. We are now a collective of nine practices, working towards a single purpose \- to expand the possibilities of what brands can achieve. At Core, we believe that change can move us forward and compel us towards new possibilities. Our wonderful people are at the centre of our success. It is essential that we keep building a high\-trust working environment that supports our people and enables them to flourish. There is no magic formula for becoming a great place to work. Instead, it involves working together, through constant change, to match the personal dreams and ambitions of our people with the aspirations of the organisation. Over the years, we have cemented our position as a great place to work, with 2025 being voted 8 Best Large Workplace in Ireland. Additionally, we are recognised as a Best Workplace for Women, and Best Workplace for Health \& Wellbeing. 2025 was our best year yet, and we're excited to have you join us and our continued success.

title: ELECTRICAL ENGINEER
description: Reporting to the Project Manager/ Engineering Manager. **Duties** * Liaising with clients and assisting in managing Electrical projects * Site surveys and Engineering inspection activities * Analysis and review of Electrical design documents * Electrical Specification writing, trouble shooting and problem solving * Provide Technical support to the Electrical Site Management Team * Manage the Electrical documentation to ensure the relevant Registers and Logs are kept up to date * Manage Electrical System handovers, with site\-based Commissioning specialists * Evaluate RFI responses and ensure engineering issues are resolved * Ensure CJK Engineering Site Management are working off the latest Electrical Specifications and Drawings * Prepare Electrical Technical Submittals according to the specifications * Populate Electrical Test Packs and collated Electrical Test record sheets for system completion * Collate Electrical O\&M Manual documentation for inclusion in the final handover documents * Ensure the Project Quality Plan and associated Electrical Inspection \& Test Plan is being followed for each Electrical system * Manage QA/QC processes * Support commissioning activities including walk\-downs, testing and handover preparations * Maintain red\-lines and review as\-builts throughout project lifecycle and handover * Mentor graduate Engineers * Collaborating with site teams, vendors, clients and consultations * Developing System Installation Work Package **Previous Experience / Qualifications** * Minimum 3\-5 years experience * Third Level qualification or currently studying Electrical Engineering or Building Services Engineering * Ability to work well within strict deadlines and on their own initiative * Good communication skills * Strong Microsoft Excel skills * Excellent attention to detail * Electrical knowledge \& experience gained from either a trade or academic background **Desirable** * Good knowledge of AutoCad / NavisWorks * Previous construction site experience * Renewable energy knowledge beneficial

title: EMEA GFCC Monitoring and Testing - Associate
description: **Job Description** Be part of a high\-impact testing team that helps protect the firm and our customers by assessing the effectiveness of financial crime controls. You will build deep expertise in financial crime risk, controls, and regulatory expectations while partnering with stakeholders across multiple business areas. This role offers strong exposure to varied processes and teams, with opportunities to broaden your skills through program work and projects. If you enjoy combining analysis, judgment, and collaboration to drive risk outcomes, this role is for you. As an EMEA GFCC Monitoring and Testing Associate in the EMEA Specialized Testing team in Dublin, you will execute specialized testing activities to assess the design and effectiveness of financial crime\-related processes and controls. You will work closely with Compliance, Operational Risk, and business stakeholders to deliver timely, well\-documented testing outcomes and clear recommendations. You will contribute to testing governance and planning activities, including inputs to the annual testing plan. You will also support regional and global initiatives as needed. **Job Responsibilities** * Execute the EMEA CCOR Specialized Monitoring and Testing Programme in line with firm expectations. * Perform testing to assess the design and operating effectiveness of controls for financial crime\-related processes. * Manage end\-to\-end testing activities, including planning, fieldwork, documentation, and reporting. * Assess business processes and controls against applicable regulatory requirements and internal standards. * Partner with Compliance, Operational Risk, and business stakeholders throughout the testing lifecycle. * Validate that action plans appropriately address identified issues and align with firm expectations. * Contribute to departmental initiatives and projects, including development of the annual testing plan. * Coordinate with global testing partners to support global testing assignments as directed. **Required Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills** * Experience in financial crime, compliance, or operational risk management, including exposure to AML, KYC, sanctions, or related areas. * Experience in audit, operations, business control management, or a similar control\-focused role. * Strong communication and influencing skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively across control functions and business stakeholders. * Demonstrated stakeholder management skills, including working with senior management. * Understanding of operational risk drivers and the ability to challenge the design and effectiveness of controls. * Ability to interpret financial regulations and assess operational compliance with regulatory requirements. * Strong analytical skills and attention to detail, including control design assessment and root cause analysis. * Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to produce clear and concise documentation. * Strong organizational skills, with the ability to prioritize work, meet deadlines, and perform well under pressure. * Confidence interacting with internal and external auditors and presenting outcomes to control functions and stakeholders. **Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills** * Testing, monitoring, or controls assessment experience within a financial services environment. * Experience contributing to risk\-based planning activities (e.g., annual testing plans). * Familiarity with structured documentation standards and issue validation practices. **ABOUT US** J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world’s most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first\-class business in a first\-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long\-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives. We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation. **About The Team** Our professionals in our Corporate Functions cover a diverse range of areas from finance and risk to human resources and marketing. Our corporate teams are an essential part of our company, ensuring that we’re setting our businesses, clients, customers and employees up for success. Our Compliance teams work globally across all lines of business to advise internal stakeholders on the impact of regulatory requirements and how to balance these with the firm’s needs. Their diverse mandate means they also provide input on new business strategies, product lines, policies, training, operational processes, risk mitigation and control.

testing to assess the design and operating effectiveness of controls for financial crime\-related processes. * Manage end\-to\-end testing activities, including planning, fieldwork, documentation, and reporting. * Assess business processes and controls against applicable regulatory requirements and internal standards. * Partner with Compliance, Operational Risk, and business stakeholders throughout the testing lifecycle. * Validate that action plans appropriately address identified issues and align with firm expectations. * Contribute to departmental initiatives and projects, including development of the annual testing plan. * Coordinate with global testing partners to support global testing assignments as directed. **Required qualifications, capabilities, and skills** * Experience in financial crime, compliance, or operational risk management, including exposure to AML, KYC, sanctions, or related areas. * Experience in audit, operations, business control management, or a similar control\-focused role. * Strong communication and influencing skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively across control functions and business stakeholders. * Demonstrated stakeholder management skills, including working with senior management. * Understanding of operational risk drivers and the ability to challenge the design and effectiveness of controls. * Ability to interpret financial regulations and assess operational compliance with regulatory requirements. * Strong analytical skills and attention to detail, including control design assessment and root cause analysis. * Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to produce clear and concise documentation. * Strong organizational skills, with the ability to prioritize work, meet deadlines, and perform well under pressure. * Confidence interacting with internal and external auditors and presenting outcomes to control functions and stakeholders. **Preferred qualifications, capabilities, and skills** * Testing, monitoring, or controls assessment experience within a financial services environment. * Experience contributing to risk\-based planning activities (e.g., annual testing plans). * Familiarity with structured documentation standards and issue validation practices. **ABOUT US** J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world’s most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first\-class business in a first\-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long\-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives. We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation. **ABOUT THE TEAM** Our professionals in our Corporate Functions cover a diverse range of areas from finance and risk to human resources and marketing. Our corporate teams are an essential part of our company, ensuring that we’re setting our businesses, clients, customers and employees up for success. Our Compliance teams work globally across all lines of business to advise internal stakeholders on the impact of regulatory requirements and how to balance these with the firm’s needs. Their diverse mandate means they also provide input on new business strategies, product lines, policies, training, operational processes, risk mitigation and control.

title: ESG Reporting Analyst
description: The ESG Reporting Analyst is responsible for coordinating and maintaining Speed Fibre Group's ESG reporting and disclosure processes. The role focuses on ensuring that all ESG data, documentation and submissions are accurate, consistent and delivered on time in line with internal and external requirements. Working across multiple functions, the role will manage established reporting frameworks, maintain ESG data and documentation, and support the delivery of key ESG outputs including GRESB, sustainability reporting and regulatory submissions. This is a detail\-oriented role with a strong focus on coordination, data management and execution. **Key Accountabilities** **ESG Systems, Certifications \& Compliance** * Maintain ISO management systems (e.g., ISO 14001\), ensuring policies, procedures and documentation are always current. * Manage structured version control and maintain accurate, audit\-ready documentation. * Support the integration of the recently acquired company into existing management systems. * Coordinate preparation for external ISO audits, gather evidence, and act as the primary operational contact for auditors and consultants. * Track audit findings, follow\-up actions, and continuous improvement items. * Monitor regulatory developments, industry trends and upcoming framework changes (e.g., GRESB updates), with support from the regulatory team. **ESG Data Management \& Analysis** * Compile, calculate and validate energy and GHG emissions data (Scope 1, 2 and 3\), as well as Parent Company data requirements, identifying gaps or inconsistencies. * Support the external GHG and energy assurance process and liaise with consultants. * Maintain evidence folders and manage a structured Teams workspace. * Track scoring changes, evidence updates, and complete annual gap analysis support. * Support ongoing improvements identified through prior submissions and feedback. **ESG Reporting \& External Assessments** * Coordinate the company’s annual GRESB submission timeline and document collection. * Manage annual questionnaire and information requests across departments. * Draft sustainability and governance sections for internal review. * Verify quantitative and narrative inputs and compile the final submission. * Collect content and metrics for the Annual Sustainability Report and related frameworks (eg SASB Index). * Coordinate with Marketing and external designers on report development and publication. * Prepare quarterly updates for Board ESG calls in collaboration with the GC and align internal documentation with the Parent Company’s responsible investment requirements. **ESG Strategy Support \& Materiality Work** * Track progress against ESG strategy actions, updating documentation and supporting internal reviews. * Provide support for the Double Materiality Assessment, including stakeholder engagement coordination, survey administration and draft materials for committees. * Collaborate with internal and external parties for the implementation of a decarbonisation strategy and actions (solar installations, efficiency upgrades, low\-carbon fleet initiatives). * Track carbon‑reduction progress, decarbonisation milestones, and energy performance metrics. **Internal Collaboration, Governance \& Communications** * Coordinate annual review and updates of ESG\-related policies, ensuring document quality and version control. * Liaise with internal teams to gather data across H\&S, Facilities, Finance, HR, Marketing and Technical functions. * Support cross‑functional activities requiring ESG evidence or analysis. * Provide accurate ESG inputs for marketing campaigns, website content, case studies and external communications. * Ensure ESG messaging aligns with corporate commitments and disclosures. **Required Qualifications/ Expertise** * 4 years’ experience in ESG, sustainability, compliance, reporting or a similar data\-driven role. * Experience supporting ESG reporting frameworks (e.g. GRESB, sustainability reports, regulatory submissions). * Strong organisational and coordination skills with the ability to manage multiple deadlines. * Excellent attention to detail and accuracy when working with data and documentation. * Strong Excel and data handling capability. * Experience managing documentation, trackers and version control processes. **Key Competencies** **Technical** * Familiarity with ISO 14001/50001 * Exposure to GRESB, SASB, SDGs, or GHG accounting * ESG data management and reporting * Document control and version management * Excel and data analysis * Reporting coordination and submission management * Evidence tracking and audit preparation support **Behavioural** * Methodical, detail\-focused and highly organised * Proactive, with strong follow\-through * Comfortable managing cyclical peak workloads * Professional, discreet and reliable * Strong cross\-functional communication and collaboration skills * Able to work with limited supervision while knowing when to escalate issues **What We Offer** * Mandatory company pension contribution. * 25 days annual leave, increasing each year up to 28 days. * Additional day off for your birthday. * Diverse and welcoming work environment – awarded Investors in Diversity Silver award in 2023 \& 2025\. * Training \& educational assistance programmes. * Monthly wellbeing initiatives. * Employee Assistance Programme. * Death in Service/Long Term Disability Benefit. Speed Fibre Group (the home of Enet and Magnet\+) is an inclusive employer, and is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for potential and existing employees with any kind of disability. Should you wish to request a reasonable accommodation we encourage you to let us know when you apply.

title: Electrical Apprentice
description: Role Overview We are a small but growing electrical company based in Dublin, delivering high\-quality electrical services across residential and commercial projects. As our workload continues to expand, we are looking to bring on a motivated and reliable First Year Electrical Apprentice to join our team and grow with the business. * Registered or eligible to register as a SOLAS electrical apprentice * Strong interest in pursuing a career as an electrician * Good work ethic and willingness to learn * Reliable, punctual, and able to work as part of a team * Safe Pass and Manual Handling certificates (preferred but not essential) * Full driving licence (an advantage but not required) Job Types: Full\-time, Apprenticeship Pay: Up to €30,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Flexitime Work Location: In person

title: Employment Solicitor
description: We are working with an elite international law firm who are seeking a Junior Associate Solicitor to join their Employment Law team within their Dublin office. **The Role** The successful candidate will join an esteemed global Employment practice, advising clients on a full spectrum of employment law matters. The candidate will advise on matters such as workforce restructuring and reductions, employment support on corporate transactions, terminations, and representation and resolution of claims before the Workplace Relations Commission and Irish courts. The candidate will also demonstrate a willingness to participate in business development while managing client relationships. **Essential Criteria** * 1\-3 PQE; * Strong background in employment law within a medium to large commercial firm; * Excellent commercial awareness; * Outstanding academics; * Negotiation skills; * Willingness to become involved in business development; * Ability to work independently and as a team. **Benefits** * Excellent salary package (commensurate with experience); * Generous benefits package; * Extensive opportunities for career development; * Excellent client base; * Hybrid working; * Inclusive and friendly culture. Please contact Nuala Darragh on \+44 7540 310 755 or email nuala.darragh@achuman.com if you would like to have a confidential discussion about this opportunity.

title: Executive Operations Associate - President's Office
description: **Company Description** It all started in sunny San Diego, California in 2004 when a visionary engineer, Fred Luddy, saw the potential to transform how we work. Fast forward to today — ServiceNow stands as a global market leader, bringing innovative AI\-enhanced technology to over 8,100 customers, including 85% of the Fortune 500®. Our intelligent cloud\-based platform seamlessly connects people, systems, and processes to empower organizations to find smarter, faster, and better ways to work. But this is just the beginning of our journey. Join us as we pursue our purpose to make the world work better for everyone. **Job Description** **What you get to do in this role:** The EMEA President's Office runs on operational precision and strategic momentum. This role exists to protect both, every single day. You will work directly with the Chief of Staff, serve as the connective tissue between the executive office and cross\-functional teams, and progressively take on operational leadership as you build trust and demonstrate judgment. This is not a traditional support role. It is a high\-accountability, high\-visibility position for someone who is already an exceptional EA and is ready for more. **Executive support (approx. 25%)** * Own the Chief of Staff's calendar with sharp scrutiny, protecting strategic time and proactively resolving conflicts before they land * Manage travel, bookings, and expense reporting end\-to\-end with zero follow\-up needed * Coordinate closely with the EA to the EMEA President to ensure non\-negotiable meeting alignment * Act as a reliable, discreet first point of contact for operational and administrative matters **Business Operations \& Project Management (approx. 75%)** * Set up and maintain President's Office operating cadences: weekly syncs, leadership forums, cross\-functional checkpoints * Track actions, milestones, and blockers across multiple workstreams, escalating proactively before issues become problems * Own the end\-to\-end planning and execution of major EMEA events: QBRs, SKO, Knowledge, Rise Together, president\-led office visits * Build and maintain SharePoint pages, OneNote structures, trackers, and insight dashboards the team actually uses * Support the development of executive\-ready presentations and briefing materials * Reinforce connection points between central and regional teams, ensuring operational clarity across a matrixed organization **Qualifications** **To be successful in this role you have:** * 5\+ years as an Executive Assistant in a fast\-paced tech or SaaS environment, ideally supporting C\-suite or a President's Office * A track record of reaching beyond your title: you have led projects, built systems, or taken ownership of things nobody asked you to own * You have felt frustrated when your role was purely logistical, and you have done something about it * Highly organized with strong written and verbal English, and the confidence to communicate with senior stakeholders directly * Fluent in SharePoint, OneNote, Excel, PowerPoint, and Smartsheet or equivalent * Global mindset and genuine curiosity about how a large, matrixed organization operates **Additional Information** **Work Personas** We approach our distributed world of work with flexibility and trust. Work personas (flexible, remote, or required in office) are categories that are assigned to ServiceNow employees depending on the nature of their work and their assigned work location. Learn more here. To determine eligibility for a work persona, ServiceNow may confirm the distance between your primary residence and the closest ServiceNow office using a third\-party service. **Equal Opportunity Employer** ServiceNow is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, national origin or nationality, ancestry, age, disability, gender identity or expression, marital status, veteran status, or any other category protected by law. In addition, all qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with legal requirements. **Accommodations** We strive to create an accessible and inclusive experience for all candidates. If you require a reasonable accommodation to complete any part of the application process, or are unable to use this online application and need an alternative method to apply, please contact globaltalentss@servicenow.com for assistance. **Export Control Regulations** For positions requiring access to controlled technology subject to export control regulations, including the U.S. Export Administration Regulations (EAR), ServiceNow may be required to obtain export control approval from government authorities for certain individuals. All employment is contingent upon ServiceNow obtaining any export license or other approval that may be required by relevant export control authorities. From Fortune. ©2025 Fortune Media IP Limited. All rights reserved. Used under license.

title: FP&A Data Analyst
description: **Why Toshiba Global Commerce Solutions?** At Toshiba, we're transforming retail with innovative solutions that tackle everyday challenges. Our mission is to turn retail visions into reality by providing expert guidance and cutting\-edge technologies. We help Retailers navigate obstacles and accelerate their growth, reimagining customer engagement. Innovation drives retail success, so we invest in future technologies to keep pace with evolving customer expectations. We work closely with clients to develop strategies and solutions that align with their goals, ensuring their business shines today and in the future. Join Toshiba Global Commerce Solutions and be a key partner in our customers' success. If you share our vision of a seamless, interactive shopping experience, let's drive this together! **JOB TITLE:** FP\&A Data Analyst **DEPARTMENT:** EU Finance Organisation **LOCATION: Ireland or Leeds, UK.** **Role Overview \- What’s ‘In\-store’?** The FP\&A Data Analyst is a critical contributor to the European FP\&A function, responsible for delivering high\-quality data analysis, reporting, and insight to support financial planning, performance management, and commercial decision making. This role is highly data and process driven, with a strong emphasis on automation, data integrity, and modern reporting to strengthen forecasting, improve data governance, and enhance the analytical capability of the finance organisation. **What Success Looks Like** * Automated, accurate, and scalable reporting that improves decision\-making. * Strong data foundations to enable improved forecasting and performance management. * Clear, actionable insights delivered consistently to senior leadership. * Measurable improvements in reporting efficiency, data governance, and process standardisation. **Key Responsibilities:** **Financial Planning \& Forecasting** * Support budgeting, forecasting, and long\-range planning cycles * Prepare, validate, and analyse large data sets to support modelling and scenario planning * Deliver variance analysis, trend insights, and performance commentary * Consolidate multi\-country inputs into a clear financial view **Reporting \& Data Visualisation** * Develop, automate, and maintain dashboards and reports using **advanced Power BI** * Ensure accuracy, consistency, and integrity of financial and operational data. * Modernize legacy reporting by implementing scalable, insight driven visualisation tools. * Partner with cross functional teams to understand reporting needs and translate them into effective BI solutions. **Data Management \& Process Improvement** * Strengthen data governance, documentation and standardisation across FP\&A processes * Identify opportunities to streamline data flows and automate manual reporting tasks. * Integrate and analyse **Salesforce** data to enhance commercial and financial insights. * Support continuous improvement initiatives to enhance forecasting accuracy and reporting efficiency. **Business Partnering \& Insight Generation** * Deliver analytical support to senior stakeholders. * Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights that support strategic and operational decisions. * Build strong relationships within Finance and Operations teams to ensure alignment on KPIs and data definitions. **What We’re Looking For** * 3–5\+ years in FP\&A, Data Analytics, Business Intelligence, or similar analytical finance roles * Experience in multinational or matrix environments is preferred * Proven ability to automate reporting, improve processes and data quality. * Experience working with Salesforce data is highly desirable. **Technical Skills** * Advanced Power BI capability (data modelling, DAX, dashboards, automation) * Strong Excel skills (Power Query, Power Pivot, advanced modelling) * Experience with ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Workday) * Strong understanding of financial statements, KPIs, and FP\&A processes **Leadership \& Behavioural Competencies** * Effective communicator able to simplify complex data for nontechnical audiences. * Structured, analytical, and detail\-oriented mindset **Education \& Qualifications** * Degree in Finance, Accounting, Analytics, or related field * *Professional certifications (ACCA, CIMA, CFA) or BI/data certifications are advantageous* **EEO** Toshiba is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer that evaluates qualified applicants without regard to protected characteristics. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Individuals who may require reasonable adjustments because of a disability for any part of the employment process should email pollyanna.hilton@toshibagcs.com to inform of any requests. **Diversity, Equity \& Inclusion** We at Toshiba Global Commerce Solutions firmly believe that our people are an integral part to the success of our customers. Furthermore, we’re committed to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion for all our people as highlighted by our 5 Core Principles (Create Outreach, Foster Belonging, Unleash Opportunity, Diverse Cultural Engagement and Culture of Transparency). With over 50\+ years of commitment to retail, we are RETAIL. Our RETAIL values (respect, empowerment, teamwork, adaptability, leadership) characterize what our employees and clients can expect when they work with us every time and all the time. We’re passionate about our customers the retail industry and becoming a more responsible company as we help create a brighter future.

title: FX Trade Operations Analyst
description: **Key Responsibilities** * Support daily trading activities and assist in resolving issues with external counterparties when required. * Ensure trade instructions are processed and communicated accurately within established deadlines. * Monitor transaction settlements and investigate any discrepancies in a timely manner. * Maintain and update records within internal systems, ensuring data accuracy across platforms. * Assist with the setup and maintenance of system data, including counterparties and accounts. * Work closely with internal teams to address operational queries and improve processes. * Build and maintain effective relationships with external service providers involved in transaction processing. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Some experience in an operations or financial services role is preferred. * Familiarity with trade processing, settlement workflows, or financial messaging systems is an advantage. * Basic understanding of financial instruments and transaction lifecycles is beneficial.

title: Fabric Sourcing Trainee
description: Because you can see how we can be better. Innovate, your way. Fabric Sourcing Trainee at Primark What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. **What You’ll Do as a** **Fabric Sourcing Trainee** We want you to feel challenged and inspired. Here, you’ll develop your skills across a range of responsibilities: * Work collaboratively with Design, Buying, Merchandising, and Quality teams to support seasonal development and prepare presentation materials. * Manage the sample library by logging, labelling, tracking sample progress, and following up with suppliers on outstanding items. * Communicate with global fabric mills and suppliers on sample requests, pricing, lead times, and technical details while supporting onboarding of new suppliers. * Maintain accurate data in sourcing systems, updating fabric charts, cost sheets, and ensuring all documentation is current and compliant. * Assist with fabric costing by gathering prices, preparing purchase requests and sample POs, and keeping sourcing trackers updated. * Research trends and product developments, preparing clear updates to support business needs and seasonal presentations. **What You’ll Get** : People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: * 27 days of leave, pension. * Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Strong organisational skills, excellent attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast‑paced environment. * Clear and professional communicator (written and verbal) with strong interpersonal skills and the confidence to present and push back professionally. * Proficient in Microsoft Excel, Outlook, and office tools, with desirable experience in textiles, sourcing, product development, or PLM/ERP systems. * Proactive, solution‑focused, and resourceful, with a strong sense of ownership and accountability. * Able to build positive relationships, work collaboratively, and adapt to changing priorities and deadlines. * Operates with integrity, sound judgement, emotional intelligence, and a high level of professionalism and trust. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. Our fashion isn’t one\-size\-fits\-all and neither is our culture. Primark promotes equal employment opportunity, we strive to create an inclusive workplace where people can be themselves, access opportunities and thrive together. **REQ ID:** JR\-6970
