title: Hotel Management Graduate Programme
description: **Ready to kick\-start your career in hospitality?** Our Manager in Training (MIT) Graduate Programme commences in September and is your chance to step into leadership with a structured 18\-month journey designed to build your confidence, skills and future. What you'll gain: * The opportunity to learn directly from experienced leaders across the business * A comprehensive understanding of hotel operations * Exposure to real challenges and responsibilities in a supportive setting * A clear pathway to develop your career with structured guidance and mentorship. * Click on the link below to find out more: https://online.flippingbook.com/view/541185829/ Who we are looking for: * Due to graduate or recently graduated with a relevant degree (Hospitality or Tourism \- however if you do not have a degree in these fields, but have experience in hospitality, we would still love to hear from you) * Excellent communication, teamwork and interpersonal skills * Ability to adapt to a fast paced environment * Passion for hospitality, guest satisfaction * Willingness to work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends * Open to travel within Ireland for the hotel rotations * Those seeking operational roles within the hotel industry (F\&B, Rooms, Front Office, M\&E and Reservations) If you are motivated, eager to learn and ready to take on new challenges, this programme will provide you with the tools and experience to succeed as a future manager. **Closing date 24th April** Please note we are unable to offer visa sponsorship for this role, so you must be authorised to work in Ireland **About us:** The MHL Hotel Collection is a collection of unique and individual hotels in key city and resort locations throughout Ireland. From the bustling city centres of Dublin, Galway, Limerick and Belfast , to picturesque and historic estates close to Wicklow and Galway City, each of our hotels has its own distinct personality and charm. *Due to the high volume of applications received, we regret that we are only able to contact candidates whose experience and profile closely match the requirements of the role. We sincerely thank all applicants for their interest and for taking the time to apply*

title: Implementation Consultant (SaaS, €75k)
description: If you thrive on delivering complex, technical solutions, enjoy building things from scratch, and are ready to take ownership of post\-sale success for enterprise clients \- this is your next challenge. You're applying for a fast\-growing, globally trusted software (Series D, $100m ARR) company helping large organisations implement critical, highly customised solutions who are looking for a **Senior Implementation Consultant** to lead end\-to\-end delivery for strategic enterprise clients You'll be working directly with senior stakeholders and cross\-functional teams. It's a **hands\-on, high\-impact post\-sale delivery** , where your technical expertise, problem\-solving, and ability to thrive in ambiguity will drive success. **What You’ll Own:** * Lead **end\-to\-end post\-sale implementations** for enterprise clients. * Manage timelines, risks, and escalations while delivering **highly customised solutions** . * Collaborate closely with Engineering, Product, Account Management, and Customer Success teams. * Translate complex customer requirements into **practical, technical implementations** . * Resolve complex technical and delivery issues, ensuring smooth, timely outcomes. * Contribute to and improve implementation methodologies and best practices. * Mentor and support junior implementation team members. **What You Bring:** * 5\+ years implementing software for **enterprise or strategic customers** . * Proven track record delivering **complex, technical, post\-sale implementations** . * Direct customer\-facing experience with **large global organisations** (e.g., Booking.com, WorldPay). * Strong experience with **APIs (RESTful)** , JSON, JavaScript, SQL, and relational databases. * Comfortable managing multiple implementations in **ambiguous, first\-time delivery scenarios** . * Strong problem\-solving skills and **technical acumen** . * Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Software, or related field preferred; formal PM certification is a plus but not essential. * Experience in SME or scale\-up environments preferred \- **mindset matters more than brand** . **The good stuff:** * **Location:** Dublin (Hybrid – Tues/Wed/Thurs in office) * **Type:** Full\-time, permanent * **Salary:** Up to €75k \& benefits If you've read this far, it means there is clearly a level of interest and you should apply (If you are the right person of course!) If you want to **own enterprise post\-sale delivery** , work with complex systems, and make an impact from day one: APPLY NOW!

title: Inside Sales Associate - Sales & Warehouse
description: **Job Description** We are currently looking for a Inside Sales Associate \- Sales \& Warehouse to join the ADI team in the Dublin. This role is a Monday\-to\-Friday 08:00\-17:00 and offers excellent, long\-term opportunities to develop a career in B2B sales \& account management. Sales \& customer focused, you will be responsible for providing excellent customer service whilst driving sales through outbound calls \& quote chasing. **JOB DUTIES:** * Deliver an excellent customer experience, maintaining strong effective relationships with customer base \& supply partners. * Working effectively with key strategic vendors and customers to promote the growth and development of strong key partnerships. * Support achieving \& delivery of Branch KPIs to identify new ways to innovate and drive growth. * Receipt of weekly stock delivery to support putting goods away in the correct warehouse location. * Receipt of customer returns and following our returns procedure to achieve customer credits and the necessary administration work * Respond to inbound calls and look for opportunities to increase the customer's request order/quotation by utilising the sales tools within our business. * Make outbound calls to proactively seek new opportunities from outbound calls, offer alternatives and utilise our promotions to proactively seek a higher return from the customer's original request. * Serving on the Trade Counter – to support serving customers on the trade counter, pick pack, and serve the customers in line with company processes. * Managing and responding to customer queries on tenders/quotations and technical queries, passing on to the relevant department where necessary. * Managing Outbound deliveries \- pick pack process and creating relevant courier paperwork. **YOU MUST HAVE:** * Prior sales/customer service experience \- preferably in a trades or wholesale environment but equally retail will be considered favourably * Flexibility to undertake all branch duties. * Ability to multi\-task * Excellent communication skills * Ability to work in a fast\-paced, demanding environment * Ability to work effectively in a team * Positive attitude **WE VALUE:** * Previous industry experience in a wholesale distributor or working title: Intern, Software Engineering
description: **Job Requisition ID \#** 25WD91936 **Position Overview** At Autodesk, we’re reimagining what’s possible. As a global leader in 3D design, engineering, and entertainment software, we help people everywhere imagine, design, and create a better world. Right now, we’re transforming how our products work by moving to the cloud—opening up exciting opportunities for collaboration, innovation, and impact. What sets us apart isn’t just our technology, but our culture. At Autodesk, you’ll find an inclusive, supportive environment where early\-career talent can grow, be creative, and make real impact. It’s one of the reasons we’ve been consistently recognized by **Fortune, Forbes, and Glassdoor as a top place to work** . Over three months in our Summer 2026 Internship Program, you’ll gain hands\-on experience, mentorship, and opportunities to grow your engineering and domain expertise—while making real contributions to Autodesk’s design, manufacturing and construction technologies. Alongside technical growth, you’ll develop soft skills and build connections with other interns—locally in your office, regionally across EMEA, and globally—through programs designed just for interns. You’ll work with a team of **Platform Services experts** in a dynamic, supportive environment, collaborating on the definition and development of core technologies for data streaming systems. As a member of the team, you will play an important role in the definition and development of core technologies for building the next generation of cloud and data platform. Along the way, you’ll gain exposure to our exciting tech stack, which includes Apache Kafka, Flink, AWS, Java, Python, APIs and backend development and DevOps tools. **Responsibilities** * Learn and apply cutting\-edge cloud and data streaming technologies * Build and test backend APIs for our cloud and data streaming platform * Prototype new features and contribute to production\-ready components * Collaborate with engineers to design, develop, and maintain scalable systems * Document and share your ideas and solutions with the team * Research and share insights on emerging technologies to help shape future development * Leverage AI vibe coding tools for Innovative solutions **Minimum Qualifications** * Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a STEM field within the EMEA region (ideally in penultimate or final year) * Please state your expected graduation month and year in your application * A growth mindset, with enthusiasm for learning new technologies and tackling challenges proactively * Detailed oriented, and passionate about solving challenging technical problems **The Ideal Candidate** * Experience with Java, and Python * Exposure to AWS, CI/CD and DevOps practices * Familiarity with Git or other version control systems * Understanding of software development principles * Interest in data streaming technologies and backend systems Learn More **About Autodesk** Welcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made. We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world. When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us! **Salary transparency** Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience, educational level, and geographic location. **Diversity \& Belonging** We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. Learn more here: https://www.autodesk.com/company/diversity\-and\-belonging

title: Investment Funds Solicitor
description: **Associate – Investment Funds (3–5 PQE)** **€90k\-110k \+ 30% bonus** **The Role** Take your chance to join a market leading Asset Management \& Investment Funds team with international reach. The team advises a global client base, including leading asset managers, fund promoters, and financial institutions, on complex and cross border fund structures and transactions. Work with this 20\+ practice as it continues growing and operating as part of a wider international network. The work is technical, international in scope, and at the forefront of regulatory and product development in the funds space. What the work looks like: * Advising on the **structuring and establishment of Irish investment funds** , including UCITS, RIAIFs, ELTIFs and QIAIFs. * Providing ongoing legal and regulatory advice to funds, management companies (UCITS ManCos, AIFMs), and asset managers. * Drafting and negotiating a wide range of fund\-related documentation and service provider agreements. * Managing matters from instruction through to completion, working closely with colleagues locally and internationally. * Supporting regulatory developments and assisting clients in navigating evolving frameworks. * Building and maintaining strong client relationships. * Contributing to client updates, thought leadership, and broader business development initiatives. **The Person** You will be: * 3\-5 PQE, qualified in Ireland/E\&W * Experienced in a leading law firm or in\-house environment * Technically strong, with excellent drafting and analytical skills. * Commercially aware, with a practical approach to problem solving. * Organised, detail oriented, and capable of managing multiple priorities. * A collaborative team player with strong communication skills. **The Perks** * Hybrid working * Generous annual leave * Health insurance * Pension scheme * Life assurance and income protection * Paid maternity and paternity leave * Client secondment opportunities **Next Steps** Hit Apply, or reach out to Mark McQuade on 0873436011 or mark@nixonmcquade.com if you’re interested in learning more.

title: Junior Business Analyst
description: **Exciting Career Opportunity \- BonsConnect Project****Location:** Open to all BSHS Locations Full time \| Specified Purpose Contract \| 12 months Remote working option available with proximity to one of our BSHS sites.**Travel across the BSHS sites will be essential.** We are delighted to announce an opportunity for a **Junior Business Analyst** and are now inviting applications from suitably qualified and experienced candidates. title: Junior Recruiter
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions, enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed in the right ways, all more confidently and more often—that’s what we call the courage to thrive. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. As a wholly owned subsidiary of U.S. Bank, Elavon is committed to building the platforms and ecosystems that help over 1\.5 million customers around the world to achieve their financial goals—no matter what they need. From transaction processing to customer service, to driving innovation and launching new products, we’re building a range of tailored payment solutions powered by the latest technology. As part of our team, you can explore what motivates and energizes your career goals: partnering with our customers, our communities, and each other. We actively uphold transparent and fair hiring practices that support individual opportunity, inclusive culture, and career mobility across all levels of our organisation. We offer meaningful opportunities for growth, a culture of inclusion, and a strong commitment to transparency and integrity in everything we do. **Job Description** ------------------- Are you a recent HR graduate or early\-career professional eager to start your recruitment career? Join our European Talent Acquisition team at U.S. Bank in Cherrywood, Co. Dublin as an **Entry\-Level Recruiter**. In this role, you’ll gain experience across the full hiring process with plenty of support and training. We’re looking for someone with a **solution\-focused attitude**, a **problem\-solving mindset**, and a strong **willingness to learn**. You’ll work in a values\-driven, inclusive environment that encourages your growth and development. ### **Key Responsibilities** * **Support Full\-Cycle Recruitment:** Assist in the end\-to\-end hiring process across Europe – from posting jobs and screening applications to scheduling interviews, managing offers, and helping new hires with onboarding. * **Collaborate with Hiring Teams:** Work closely with Hiring Managers and HR colleagues to understand role requirements and help develop effective hiring strategies. You’ll learn how to advise and support managers in finding the right talent. * **Source \& Attract Candidates:** Use job boards, social media (e.g., LinkedIn), referrals, and other creative methods to find and engage potential candidates. Over time, you will build skills in **talent sourcing** and learn to map out where to find the best people. * **Ensure Great Candidate Experience:** Provide clear communication and timely feedback to candidates at each step. Help coordinate structured interviews and make sure every candidate feels respected and informed throughout the process. * **Data \& Process Management:** Maintain accurate records in our Applicant Tracking System (Workday). Learn to track key recruitment metrics (like time\-to\-hire) and use data to suggest improvements. Ensure all recruitment activities follow our policies and European legal requirements, with guidance from the team. ### **Skills \& Experience** * **Education:** Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business, or a related field. **Recent graduates** are welcome – this role is designed to help you grow. * **Communication \& People Skills:** Strong communication skills and a friendly, professional attitude. You’re comfortable reaching out to new people and can build positive relationships with colleagues and candidates. * **Organised \& Detail\-Oriented:** Good organisational skills to manage multiple tasks or job openings at once. You pay attention to details – whether it’s scheduling interviews or entering candidate data – and you ask questions when you’re unsure. * **Tech\-Savvy \& Quick Learner:** Comfortable learning new systems and tools. Experience with any Applicant Tracking System (e.g., Workday) or MS Office is a bonus. More importantly, you’re eager to **learn new technologies** and follow processes carefully. * **Problem\-Solving Mindset:** A **solution\-focused, can\-do attitude**. When faced with a challenge, you stay positive and think of ways to solve it. You’re adaptable and open to feedback, always looking to improve. ### **Desirable (Nice\-to\-Have)** * **Analytical Interest:** Comfort with data or any experience from your studies using Excel or analytics tools. title: Junior Supply Chain Consultant - September 2026
description: **Job Description** **About the role** **Kickstart your consulting career!** Get ready to launch your career in consulting, and join us! You’ll have a spot in our supply chain training boot camp at the beginning of September. You’ll sharpen your consulting expertise alongside fellow new starters from around the globe. Our training program covers everything from essential soft skills to advanced supply chain management to becoming a kick\-ass Supply Chain Consultant. Afterwards, you're ready to start on one of our running projects. **What can you do at Bluecrux?** * You'll start immediately in a visible, autonomous role as a project member in one of our running value chain projects in industries such as life sciences, chemicals, food and beverages, and manufacturing. * You'll see yourself working on and contributing to the main project phases. Here's an idea of what to expect: + Analyze and Optimize: You will always analyze, map, and conduct existing Supply Chain and Operations business processes. + Collaborate: Work closely with clients to understand their needs and make a real impact. Think about preparing and conducting project interviews to understand the client's needs better. You will become aware of the customer organization and be part of the change in their organization and ways of working. + Track and report: You'll track KPIs and report them accurately to the customer project team, ensuring clear, actionable insights. + Document and deliver: You create the most important customer and project deliverables, such as top\-notch project notes, guides and training material. * Here’s how you’ll continuously be making an impact: + Tell us, what’s your flavour? All of our projects share a common transformation mission, and once you join a project, you’ll be introduced to different extra layers of specialisation to add value. We will encourage you to explore and accelerate in Technology, Process knowledge, Data or Change Management. **Job Requirements** **About you** * You will graduate before September 2026 and want to start your career at the beginning of September 2026\. * You see yourself thriving in the world of value chain transformation and are interested in how Technology, Business Processes, Data Analytics, and Change management create a flourishing ecosystem of solutions. * You feel connected with and are ready to represent our company values: dig deep, own it, come together, move fast and be kind! * Above all, we believe in your potential: + You can demonstrate profound logical reasoning, and you are resourceful in detailing solutions and considerate about their consequences. + You're a team worker and player who can perform well without much supervision and who is committed to your own objectives. + You're open to feedback and always look for ways to enhance collaboration. + You like to be challenged daily, show proactive ownership and, above all, thrive in a fast\-paced environment. * Some hard skills: + Ideally, you have had an introduction to Supply Chain Processes in a minor or major within a master’s degree program, such as business engineering, civil engineering—operational research, applied economics, mechanical engineering or an MBA program. + You are fluent in English, the official work language in all of our offices. Mastery of any other language is a definite plus. + Willingness and eligibility to travel globally and to work in a hybrid working model. **Why join us?** * A kickstart to your career through our Supply Chain Bootcamp training program, at the beginning of September. You will learn the ins \& outs of Supply Chain Management: Demand Planning, S\&OP and Financial Planning, Supply Planning, Inventory Management, Master Data Management, Supply Chain Modelling and Planning Tools. This will allow you to onboard into our projects with a backpack full of expertise \& skills! * We know you by your first name, and don't let titles or levels withhold us from being truly aware of your talents. * You'll work on international projects and travel to client sites. From the start, you will gain exposure and recognition for every achievement at Bluecrux. * A Great Place To Work with lots of growth opportunities. We encourage initiative, so you’ll learn a lot quickly. Sometimes, you’ll fail fast, but we’ll help you to get up. We’ll enhance your personal and professional skills, and you can count on a great, supportive team of hands\-on, inspiring and enthusiastic supply chain colleagues. * Team events and fun include yearly Bluecrux weekends, last Friday meetings with drinks, team building, and sports activities. * Enjoy a flexible \& hybrid work environment, combining remote work, office presence and onsite client visits. * A competitive salary package, bonus program, net allowances, a smartphone with phone subscription and other benefits (such as a fridge full of drinks \& healthy snacks). **About us** At Bluecrux, we transform supply chains into smart, efficient value chains through a blend of expert consulting and cutting\-edge technology. We collaborate closely with industry leaders like Johnson \& Johnson, GSK, Bridgestone, AkzoNobel, Beiersdorf, Oatly, and many more – creating partnerships that drive meaningful impact. With a global presence in Europe, the US and APAC, we're committed to shaping the future of value chains, one success story at a time.

landscape installation * Working on commercial landscapes, streetscapes, rooftop projects and sports facilities * Supporting experienced team members while learning practical site skills This is varied, hands\-on outdoor work with the opportunity to gain valuable experience across a wide range of projects. **Where You’ll Work** Most of our work is based in the **Dublin area**, with occasional work in other parts of the country. **What We’re Looking For** We are looking for someone who is: * Interested in building a long\-term career * Comfortable working outdoors in a physical role * Working at height experience * Soft or hard landscaping experience * Construction or outdoor work experience **Working Hours** * Typical hours: **7:30am – 4:00pm** * Seasonal variability applies * title: Legal PA, Litigation & Dispute Management - Dublin
description: We are Eversheds Sutherland, a global law firm, with more than 5,000 people across over 30 countries. We’re full\-service with deep niche and sector experience. Whatever challenge, wherever in the world, we’re equipped and ready to meet it. We live our values, we’re purposeful and purpose\-led. So although the world is fast\-moving and rapidly changing, we see it as a place where everyone can thrive. We’re ambitious for our clients, our communities – and for you. Whether you’re starting out on your career or well established, whether you’re a lawyer or in business services. If you’re looking for what’s next, we are too. **What to expect** As a Legal PA within our Practice Group Support team, your role is pivotal. You’ll provide confidential, professional and proactive support to a designated group of stakeholders. Confidence, effective communication and influential skills are essential. We value an assertive and forward\-thinking approach. Here’s what to expect: **Diverse responsibilities:** Our Legal PAs handle a wide range of tasks, supporting various aspects of our business. Empowerment and mentorship: You’ll lead by example, fostering ongoing personal and professional development among junior colleagues and peers. **Continuous learning:** We invest in your professional development through our development plans. We offer personalised mentoring and coaching, technical training and soft skills courses to boost motivation and success in your career path. **Culture of feedback:** Our culture thrives on continuous feedback that builds confidence, development and recognition of achievements. **Pro Bono, Community and Diversity \& Inclusion (D\&I) opportunities:** Beyond your daily tasks, we encourage you to contribute to Pro Bono, Community and D\&I initiatives. Make a difference by using your skills to benefit our communities. **Ambition for people, clients and communities:** Our ambition extends beyond business success. We are equally committed to the growth and well\-being of our people, clients and the broader community. **Staying ahead:** Collaborate closely with lawyers, take ownership of tasks and exceed business expectations, anticipate challenges, adapt to changing technologies. in the legal sector. For talented, ambitious individuals there's no better place to be working in the law industry. **Role and key responsibilities** **Stakeholder engagement:** Conduct regular one to one meetings with stakeholders to discuss actions, priorities, expectations and future planning. Attend team meetings to stay informed and contribute effectively. Act as a liaison point for both internal and external clients, draft clear and concise replies to incoming correspondence. Assist with client queries during stakeholder absences. Maintain a flexible approach and adopt hybrid working to accommodate stakeholder and client needs. **Adaptability and collaboration:** Demonstrate client service excellence by being responsive to stakeholder requests. Support the wider PA team, covering for team members during absences or when required to ensure continued resource. **Efficient mailbox, diary and travel management:** Proactively manage mailboxes and diaries including travel coordination. Anticipate future needs and take initiative to resolve conflicts. **Meeting organisation and coordination:** Draft agendas and prepare for internal and external meetings. Ensure necessary equipment and refreshments for both physical and virtual meetings using available technology and resources. **Workflow management and delegation:** Own the end\-to\-end process, be accountable for successful conclusion of all stakeholder requests. Delegate tasks effectively across dedicated support teams such as billing, finance, team assistants, document production to meet expectations and deadlines in line with the Scope of Service. **Document management:** Undertake urgent and short document work. Collaborate with the Document Production Team for larger documents utilising our dedicated software. **Matter management:** Manage the entire client and matter opening process including electronic filing, collaborating with stakeholders and other business support teams. **Financial administration and compliance:** Provide financial administrative support to stakeholders, coordinating with billing and finance teams, ensuring compliance with legal, firm\-wide and practice\-specific policies and procedures. **Pitch and presentation creation:** Collaborate with the document production team to create impactful pitches and PowerPoint presentations for stakeholders. **Marketing and business development:** Coordinate and organise events, create event material, digital working lists and communicate with attendees to ensure a seamless end to end process. **Championing process improvements:** Be an advocate for process changes within the organisation, influence decisions and be a part of the solution. **Efficient administration:** Manage electronic filing, records maintenance and expenses effectively using the firm's designated software. **Technology utilisation:** Leverage the firm’s technology as a key business tool for managing, updating, reporting and research. Create and maintain data sites for efficient collaboration. Seek opportunities to embrace technologies that improve our ways of working and drive productivity in day\-to\-day activities. **Skills and experience** We are looking for: * Experience in a similar environment: Experience working within a comparable professional setting, providing seamless adaptation to our busy environment. * Client service excellence: Strive to exceed client expectations by delivering outstanding service. Anticipate client needs and proactively address any issues. * Problem\-solving and initiative: Be a proactive problem solver. Anticipate challenges, take initiative and implement solutions efficiently. Aspire for excellence in all tasks. Show dedication and commitment to achieving strategic goals. * Effective communication: Demonstrate excellent communication skills when liaising with stakeholders and clients. Your ability to convey information clearly and professionally is essential. * Delegation and ownership: Delegate tasks across dedicated support teams while maintaining overall ownership of transactional activities. * High\-level support: Provide exceptional PA support to senior stakeholders, including managing calendars, organising meetings and handling confidential information. * Relationship building: Cultivate strong relationships both internally and externally. Collaborate effectively with colleagues and clients. * Task management and risk awareness: Skilfully manage tasks with expertise, seeking ways to enhance efficiency and quality. Balance priorities and stay alert to risks, ensuring tasks are completed on time and stakeholder expectations are met. * Flexibility: Demonstrate flexibility and embrace change. Adapt seamlessly to evolving work environments. * Microsoft applications proficiency: Demonstrate intermediate to advanced skills in Microsoft applications including Outlook, Teams and Copilot. * Team collaboration: Contribute to a positive team environment. Offer support to colleagues when needed. **Diversity \& Inclusion** At Eversheds Sutherland, “Inclusive” is a core business value. We bring together different skillsets, global mindsets and approaches. We foster diversity of thought and the freedom to put ideas into action. We have an inherent respect for the individual. We have a strong belief in collaboration and teamwork. Sharing ideas, asking questions, solving challenges and meeting our clients’ goals: together. We want all our people to thrive at work and reach their full potential and we work hard to continue to build a diverse and inclusive culture, monitor and report on our progress and impact, and develop our approach. This is reflected in our policies, systems and processes, and in our work with diversity membership organisations. Many of our people work flexibly in some way and we are open to considering how we can accommodate flexible working arrangements alongside role requirements. If this is important to you, please talk to us about it during the recruitment process. We want you to perform at your best during our recruitment process. If there is any adjustment or support you need, please contact us so we can discuss how we can best assist you. **For you,** **For your success.** **And what's next.**

title: Maintenance Technician
description: Requisition ID: 73816 Date: Mar 31, 2026 Location: Dublin, L, IE Department: Operations Description: At West, we’re a dedicated team that is connected by a purpose to improve patient lives that has been at the center of our Company for more than a century. Our story began when Herman O. West solved the problem of supplying penicillin in mass quantities to the US Government during World War 2\. Through our work to deliver thousands of life\-saving and life\-enhancing injectable medicines to millions of patients daily, West’s indelible mark on the healthcare industry has just begun. A name started our story. How will yours help write our future? There’s no better place to join an inclusive community of professionals with opportunities for lifelong learning, growth and development. Supported by benefit programs, we empower the physical, mental, emotional and financial health of our team members and their families. We believe in giving back to help those in need in the communities where we live and work. And are equally committed to creating a healthier environment and planet through our sustainability efforts. #### **Job Summary** Manages the maintenance and repair of equipment in operation at West. Liaises with external contractors for the maintenance and repair of same. The Maintenance Electrician is also responsible for using the preventative maintenance system to pre\-determined guidelines. Reporting directly to the Maintenance Lead Technician. Become a mentor to apprentice electricians and support their development.**Essential Duties and Responsibilities** ----------------------------------------- * Responsible for the maintenance and breakdown repair of moulding equipment (including among others, moulding machines, automation/robots, water heaters, oil heaters, conveyors, and hot runner controllers). * Work with the Production Managers to troubleshoot equipment/plant problems. * Establishes relationships with the customers and suppliers in pursuit of continuous improvement and on\-going business development. * Attends, schedules, and facilitates internal meetings to help establish priorities and assign tasks * Solves, in conjunction with the Quality department, customer related problems/issues as needed * In conjunction with approved vendors, research new processes and/or systems for maintenance/manufacture of equipment and implement new processes and systems as developed under the direction of the Operations Manager. * Instructs others, formally or informally, regarding manufacturing related skills or knowledge and assist in the training of employees on new processes or systems introduced to the company * Compliance to all site Environmental, Health and Safety requirements, training, and regulations * Support and contribute to Lean Sigma programs and activities towards delivery of the set target * Perform additional duties at the request of the direct manager **Education** ------------- * Associate's Degree Time served QQI Level 6 Advanced Certificate Craft Electrical required * Bachelor's Degree Engineering preferred **Work Experience** ------------------- * Minimum 3 years experience is required in troubleshooting various manufacturing equipment. **Preferred Knowledge, Skills and Abilities** --------------------------------------------- * Sound knowledge of CMMS and MS Office * Any other certifications are a plus. * Support and contribute to Lean Sigma programs and activities towards delivery of the set target. * Able to always comply with the company’s safety policy. * Able to always comply with the company’s quality policy. * Knowledge of PLC Programming and electrical work * Ability to read electrical, mechanical, and hydraulic/pneumatic drawings is essential * Ability to interact with suppliers, customers, and external contractors **Travel Requirements** ----------------------- 5%: Up to 13 business days per year**Physical Requirements** ------------------------- Medium\-Exerting up to 50lb/22kg of force occasionally and/or up to 20lbs/9kg of force frequently, and/or up to 10lbs/4kgs of force constantly to move objects.**Additional Requirements** --------------------------- * Must be able to lift 35 lbs. continuously and up to 75 lbs. occasionally, with or without assistance. **Shift** --------- 2 cycle shift pattern (shifts are 8am \- 8pm \& include every 2nd weekend) \#LI\-MMM \#LI\-Onsite West is an equal opportunity employer and we value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. If you have a special need that requires accommodation in order to apply to West, please send an email to Apply.Accommodation@westpharma.com. Where permitted by law, an offer of employment with West Pharmaceutical Services, or any of its subsidiary or affiliate companies, is contingent upon the satisfactory completion of background screening.

title: Managing Consultant
description: **Job Description** **MANAGING CONSULTANT** **Dublin** **Fixed Term Contract / Permanent** **Role Summary** This is a fantastic opportunity to join our fast\-growing Consulting Business working with some of the world’s leading financial institutions. As a **Managing Consultant** , the successful candidate will work directly with our clients to ensure they receive the quality consulting services that Delta Capita (DC) offers. The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of financial services, exceptional problem\-solving skills, project management and business analysis experience, the ability to manage, motivate and inspire others, and a proven track record of delivering successful client engagements. You will have deep change management experience and prior success as a management consultant or within a change role in a financial services institution. To know more about our Consulting services, click here Consulting \- Delta Capita. **Job Requirements** **The Role and Responsibilities** This role is ideal for someone with the right mix of consulting and client management experience as well as having an entrepreneurial spirit. You will combine project delivery know\-how, a can\-do attitude, and a strong knowledge of Financial Services. **Project responsibilities include;** * Coordinate delivery of project tasks from initiation to closure, including definition of project scope and objectives, budgetary considerations, resource allocation \& training, formal documentation, delivery, monitoring and control, reporting and delivery assurance. * Day to day management and central point of contact for the project * Manage changes to the project scope, project schedule and project costs using appropriate verification techniques * Create and maintain all project documentation e.g., Terms of reference/ project charters, project plans, status reports. * Establish and maintain governance processes including stakeholder mapping, governance forums (e.g. Operating \& Steering Committees) and all supporting documentation * Design \& deliver documentation to support the successful operational implementation * Identify opportunities to streamline operational process relevant to the project * Manage and oversee project plan and RAID log (Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies) * Actively manage risks and issues, escalating to senior management and client stakeholders where required to ensure they are appropriately and quickly addressed, documenting as ‘lessons learnt’ once resolved * Build and manage relationships with project stakeholders, ensuring appropriate level and frequency of communication * Ensure decisions and outcomes are formally documented via minutes, sign\-offs, project documentation or any other client specific tools * Define, gather and collate meaningful KPIs and dashboards for management reporting * Undertake other Transformation \& Change lifecycle roles/responsibilities as required **As An Experienced Managing Consultant, You Will Have** * Proven Consulting experience or alternatively in Project Management or Business Analysis within Financial Services. * Change management experience: Strategy, Design, Analysis, Project Management, Build Testing, Go\-Live. * Understanding of Financial Services products and lifecycle events – Trade Finance experience is desired * Risk and control awareness in a banking environment * Experience managing projects and team members supporting successful project/programme deliveries. * Good knowledge of the capital markets and retail banking eco\-system and participants * You will come from a management consultant background or within a change role in a financial services institution. You must have a blend of both Financial Services knowledge and soft skills across influence, listening, presentation and team management. * As an experienced Consultant, you will be expected to be comfortable working on an independent basis and managing your own workload with minimal supervision. * You will also be responsible for helping to grow our business by supporting ongoing operations, RFPs and helping to manage junior consultants. **How We Work** Delta Capita is an equal opportunity employer. We positively encourage applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of age, colour, disability, national origin, ancestry, race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, veteran status, genetic information, or any other status protected by applicable law. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. If you require any reasonable adjustments through your interview process, please use the designated space within the application questionnaire. This is fixed term contract position located in Dublin, Ireland. As the selection and interview process is ongoing, please submit your application as soon as possible. If your profile is selected, a member of our team will contact you within 4 weeks. For this role, a valid working permit for Ireland is mandatory. **Who We Are** Delta Capita Group (a member of the Prytek Group) is a global managed services, consulting, and solutions provider with a unique combination of experience in Financial Services and technology innovation capability. Our mission is to reinvent the financial services value chain providing technology based mutualized services for financial institutions for non\-differentiating services. **Our 3 Offerings Are** * Managed Services * Consulting \& Solutions * Technology To know more about Delta Capita and our culture click here: Working at DC \- Delta Capita.

title: Microsoft SharePoint Architect
description: ### **Description** As we continue to experience rapid growth, we are seeking a highly skilled SharePoint Architect to join our team. The successful candidate will provide expert\-level support, administration, and consultancy across Microsoft SharePoint environments both on\-premises (SharePoint Server 2013/2016/2019 \& Subscription Edition) and SharePoint Online. The role combines advanced troubleshooting and incident resolution with the delivery of enhancements and change for our customers. Working closely with business stakeholders and technical teams, the consultant will ensure SharePoint solutions are optimised, secure, and aligned with business requirements. ### **Key Responsibilities** * Troubleshoot complex technical issues across SharePoint, Microsoft 365, Teams, and integrated systems; perform root cause analysis for recurring issues and implement permanent fixes. * Manage and maintain (SharePoint Server 2013/2016/2019 \& Subscription Edition), including site collections, permissions, content databases, and service applications. * Configure, monitor, and optimise performance, security, and availability. * Support migration projects (on\-premises to SharePoint Online, tenant\-to\-tenant, or version upgrades). * Develop and support custom solutions using SharePoint Framework (SPFx), TypeScript, React, and modern web technologies. * Build and maintain solutions using Power Platform (Power Automate, Power Apps, Power BI). ### **Skills, Knowledge and Expertise** **Required skills \& experience** * Minimum 5\-8 years' hands\-on experience with Microsoft SharePoint, including architecture, service applications, and administration. * SP data migrations (experience of Sharegate \- who also has good experience of PP/CE) * Proficiency across the Microsoft 365 ecosystem (Teams, OneDrive, Exchange Online). * Experience with PowerShell scripting for automation and troubleshooting. * Experience working within ticketing systems and SLA\-driven environments. **Desirable skills \& experience** * 5\+ years' experience with Power Platform technologies (Power Automate, Power Apps, Power BI). * Experience with migration tooling such as Sharegate, AvePoint Fly, or LinkFixer Advanced. * Experience with Teams Phone (Voice). ### **About Codec** Established over 40 years ago, Codec is a multi\-award\-winning Microsoft Solutions Partner, trusted by public and private sector organisations to deliver impactful digital transformation. With offices in **Dublin**, **Belfast**, **Cologne**, and across **Germany**, our team of over **300 experts** specialises in harnessing the full potential of Microsoft technologies—including **Dynamics 365**, **Power Platform**, **Azure**, and **Copilot**—to drive innovation, automation, and operational excellence. Codec combines deep industry expertise with a proven track record to help organisations unlock the power of AI and modern cloud solutions Though originally best known as a hardware reseller, since 2010 Codec has focused on building an outstanding software consulting business with a team of experts implementing enterprise solutions using products from Microsoft and Oracle. Our customer base, revenues and average contract values have grown steadily year on year and we continue to invest in the provision of new product and service offerings. Our team is a careful mix of recent graduates and experienced professionals from a variety of diverse cultural backgrounds. We are a melting pot of seasoned consultants supported by the best talent from the local universities. We have a thriving placement student academy and graduate recruitment programme which grows year on year. This company is an equal opportunities employer

title: Operational Risk Associate
description: Pantheon has been at the forefront of private markets investing for more than 40 years, earning a reputation for an innovative approach to investing in secondaries, co\-investments, and primary fund investments, as well as capital formation across commingled funds, evergreen vehicles and customized solutions. Our specialist investment capabilities span multiple strategies across private equity, infrastructure and real assets, and private credit. Through our collaborative and committed culture, we find new ways to solve complex problems together and deliver innovative investment opportunities across private markets. Pantheon currently manages approximately $82\.3 billion in AUM across all its strategies, serving more than 750 institutional and 638 private wealth clients worldwide For further details please visit www.pantheon.com **Purpose of Role** The Operational Risk Associate will be part of the wider Enterprise Risk / Operational Risk function and support the team in further developing and managing operational risks across all business functions. The position will report to the Head of Enterprise/Operational Risk. **Key Responsibilities** * Support the development and implementation of Pantheon's global operational risk frameworks and policies and procedures for adoption by Pantheon Group entities * Support the ISAE 3402/SSAE 16 annual processes including completion of the document, drafting of controls wording and providing external auditor with supporting information. * Support process and control reviews to identify risks and issues in Pantheon's control environment. Reviews will include identified areas of risk, monitoring programme and new business processes. * Manage Error/Incident reporting process including reporting to management and resolution of any issues. Ensure monitoring procedures are in line with Pantheon's risk policies. Direct investigation of serious error or breaches and make recommendations for change to management * Co\-ordinate and assist in providing risk oversight and governance across third party supplier relationships of all Pantheon Group entities * Support business continuity process including BCP and testing programme. * Support to manage various risk\-related frameworks such as the data privacy / data protection, vendor risk, Insurance risk, and assist with any client / business related queries * Support and further develop a centralised library of processes, risks and controls in a strategic risk system **Skills** **and Experience Required** * Degree level education * Experience of working in a risk function ideally in an investment banking, asset management, private equity environment * Understanding of investment management and products * Ability to work on own initiative and in team * Ability to identify when issues should be escalated for further discussion * Controls awareness * Keen problem\-solving skills, ability to offer solutions to issues \#LI\-SB1 **This job description is not to be construed as an exhaustive statement of duties, responsibilities, or requirements. You may be required to perform other job\-related duties as reasonably requested by your manager.** **Pantheon is an Equal Opportunities employer, we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce so if you're excited about this role but your past experience doesn't perfectly align we'd still encourage you to apply.**

title: Operations Assistant
company: Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland (RCSI)
description: **Title:** Operations Assistant **Department:** Faculty of Radiologists and Radiation Oncologists **Tenure:** Permanent \- Full\-time **Location:** RCSI, St Stephen’s Green, Dublin 2 **Reporting to:** Dean of the Faculty or nominee **About the post** This post is designed to provide a comprehensive administrative support service to the Dean, Honorary Secretary and Officers of the Board of the Faculty of Radiologists and Radiation Oncologists with specific emphasis on the Faculty training programmes. This role is an integral part of the Faculty ideally suited for an ambitious individual to support trainees on their journey from application to graduation along with ad\-hoc events and projects within the Faculty. **Duties include:** * Act as the key point of contact for trainees, fellows, training coordinators, clients and stakeholders * Support trainee recruitment to the programmes through response to enquiries, liaising with prospective trainees, follow up with applicants and management of the online training programme systems * Assisting with the organisation and operation of scientific meetings, functions, conferring ceremonies, board and committee meetings and other events associated with the Faculty. * Assisting with organising and running of training courses, modules, interviews and assessments * Work collaboratively with the National Training Coordinators, lecturers, trainees and other Operations Assistants to ensure effective running of courses and delivery of a highly flexible, responsive and effective service to all * Assist with preparing documentation for accreditation and site visits for both radiation oncology and diagnostic radiology training programmes * Maintain training passports for trainees and ensure these are up to date for all individuals, ensuring all mandatory courses completed for CSCST purposes * Maintain the educational calendar for all Faculty courses throughout the year and updating MedHub with training courses, modules, lectures and recordings * Attend all operational educational meetings and discuss any changes on the calendar and manage and coordinate ongoing requirements for courses as they arise * Preparing training programme specialty rotations, lecture schedules, recordings as required. * Provision of assistance to tutors in the set up of zoom and uploading of tutors presentations for lecture purposes in the lecture theatre/tutorial room * Updating Hospital Buddy with dates for lectures and circulation details for lectures to both tutors and SpRs * Updating MedHub with tutors slides for revision purposes for SpRs * Support the delivery of online modules, courses, webinars and conferences in line with faculty requirements as they arise * Collating programme outcomes into documented reports in support of programmatic review and continuous quality improvement * Preparation and collation of the Faculty Annual Report * Providing assistance to the Executive Officer * Reviewing and updating the Faculty website with relevant information, reports, publications, news etc * Collation and circulation of all information and documentation for meetings. Preparing and taking minutes as required and keeping Sharepoint updated * Assist with the preparation and completion of circulars for Fellows and trainees * Assist with processing of expense claims for board and committee members, lecturers, visiting speakers, trainee discretionary fund in line with Faculty, RCSI and NDTP policies * Coordinate the ordering and supply of stationery and materials for the faculty within the budget limits * Undergo programmes of training and development as may be required from time to time * Completion of the annual mandatory staff courses and training in technology * Complying with statutory legislation and department rules and requirements in furtherance of own and general staff welfare and safety * Representing the best interests of the Faculty of Radiologists and Radiation Oncologists and RCSI at all times * Performing such other duties appropriate to the post as may arise from time to time **Person specification** * Strong communication and interpersonal skills appropriate to audience and situation * Have strong customer service skills and communicate with high level of diplomacy and confidentiality * Ability and sensitivity to operate and liaise with busy and highly skilled professionals with differing priorities, geographic bases etc. * Team player – ability to work collaboratively alongside others to maximise individuals and team effectiveness in order to achieve required service outputs * Highly organised with an acute attention to detail and follow through, giving full consideration to all aspects of the task / project including potential issues and wider impact on Faculty activities and functions * Excellent computerised administration skills * Excellent time and management skills * Ability to make decisions, identify, prioritise workload and meet deadlines and escalate problems when necessary * Ability to work on own initiative * Can operate flexibly within a busy and changing environment; can shift focus when required, act decisively and prioritise several tasks to meet deadlines * Flexibility with working hours when required **Knowledge and experience (essential):** * Understanding of accounting and budgets * Proficient in administrative and educational programmes and platforms such as Microsoft Office Suite, Panopto, Quercus, Zoom, social medial platform X etc **Knowledge and experience (desirable):** * Understanding of medical working environment and legal parameters * Proven track record in a similar role would be advantageous ***We are all too aware that imposter syndrome and the confidence gap can sometimes stop fantastic candidates putting themselves forward, so please do submit an application — we’d love to hear from you.***

title: Operations Risk & Resilience Placement - 2026 Work Placement
description: **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Dublin and Cork.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. The Operations Consulting Team Supports Clients Across The Full Asset Lifecycle—from Acquisition And Planning Through To Operation And Disposal—by Addressing The Interdependencies Between People, Processes, Systems, And Assets. The Discipline Delivers a Broad And Integrated Set Of Services, Including * Operational Strategy \& Transformation: Helping clients align operational execution with strategic goals, including digital transformation initiatives and process improvement * Governance, Risk \& Compliance (GRC): Support the delivery of effective compliance and risk management programmes to identify, manage and mitigate business and digital related risk * Operational Readiness \& Performance Improvement: Ensuring systems, teams, and processes are prepared for launch or transition, and continuously optimised for performance * Digital Enablement: Leveraging tools like BIM, Digital Twins, and analytics platforms to improve operational visibility and decision\-making **The Opportunity** Arup Ireland are seeking talented students to join our Cork or Dublin team. We look for students who are ready to jump into the professional world; diverse individuals with inquisitive minds and a desire to challenge. You will work alongside skilled professionals and contribute to the success of real projects. You will also enjoy learning sessions with technical experts within your team, working on local projects and all kinds of social events. You will be working with a team of Operations Consulting Services professionals from various offices. The Operations Consulting team embodies Arup’s Total Design philosophy by integrating people, processes, technology, and physical assets into cohesive operational strategies that are embedded from concept through to implementation. The team works with a variety of public sector organisations, including Transport Infrastructure Ireland. With reference to our Risk \& Resilience service within Operations Consulting, it exemplifies Arup’s Total Design by embedding resilient governance frameworks, proactive risk intelligence, and compliance assurance into the operational phase of projects—ensuring that systems, processes, and people are holistically integrated. This is an office\-based role, with successful candidates expected to work 5 days per week in the Arup office in Cork or Dublin. **As a work placement student in the Operations team at Arup, you will:** * Support turning technical recommendations into operational plans and project deliverables with defined sustainability outcomes for our clients * Practice creative applications of technology to develop design solutions * Develop communication skills, including report writing and visual representations * Attend and participate in meetings – listen, ask questions * Be involved in discussions with team members to gain knowledge \& experience * Undertake analytical research to develop, document, present concepts and design ideas * Review project documentation, applicable codes and standards to assist with preparation of reports, presentations, drawings and/or sketches * Give structure to ideas – lots of “taking a first pass” at spreadsheets, reports and analyses for discussion later rather than instruction **Does this role inspire you?** We're Looking For Students Who Are Currently Studying For a Relevant Bachelor’s Degree Level Or Be Currently Undertaking a Post\-graduate Level Qualification In a Relevant Discipline, Including, But Not Limited To * Governance \& Compliance Management * Business in Operations Management * Management (Operations \& Supply Chain) * Supply Chain Operations * Business Information Systems * Computer Systems Engineering * Cybersecurity **We Also Value** * Experience of producing technical presentations and reports, knowledge sharing with peers * History of academic research, technical presentations, and technical report writing * Experience with any of the following: GRC software solutions, Cybersecurity solutions, Microsoft Visio, PowerPoint, or Project, or analysis packages * Knowledge of ISO Assurance Frameworks such as 27001 or 55001 and Data Privacy regulations * Evidence of teamwork and leadership in academic or extracurricular field * The ability to prioritise effectively, balance assigned work and deliver to deadlines **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in investing in our people and supporting their growth from day one. As a trust\-owned company, we're driven by our purpose and values, creating a collaborative and rewarding environment for our employees. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth \& Development: Kickstart your career with our structured program, benefit from continuous learning opportunities, and receive guidance from experienced mentors. * Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. If you need any assistance or adjustments, please visit our Candidate Support page for contact information and assistance. We're happy to help. **Please note this role is only suitable for those who are in full time education and are looking for a placement for the summer months or for those who are required to complete a placement as part of their course requirements.** **If you have recently graduated, please apply to one of our '2026 Graduate Programme' roles.** \#IREIntern **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: P2P Analyst
description: **Title** : P2P Analyst **Location:** Kildare **Contract:** Permanent Duties * Maintain and update supplier, customer, pricing, and GL master data in Oracle * Ensure data accuracy, governance standards, and audit trails are adhered to * Manage master data requests and resolve queries from internal teams * Process supplier invoices (PO \& non\-PO), credit notes, and intercompany transactions * Perform supplier reconciliations and support month\-end activities * Operate invoice scanning/validation tools and handle invoice rejections * Support purchasing activities (PO creation, requisitions, pricing updates) * Enforce procurement policies, including “No PO No Pay” compliance * Monitor inboxes (Master Data \& Purchasing) and respond to queries * Assist with reporting, testing, audits, and process improvement initiatives * Provide cover for P2P helpdesk and support wider finance operations Requirements * 3\+ years’ experience in Master Data, Accounts Payable, or Procure\-to\-Pay (P2P) * Strong understanding of finance operations, including invoicing, reconciliations, and purchasing processes * Experience working with ERP systems (ideally Oracle) * Good working knowledge of Excel and Microsoft Office ***Desirable*** * Accounting Technician qualification (or qualified by experience) * Experience with invoice scanning tools / automation (e.g. OCR, RPA) - If you are interested in this role, please apply below or reach out to Sarah Friel in Lex Consultancy.

title: PARK RANGER
description: Professional and Technical PARK RANGER €764\.92 \- €805\.40 Advertised Date: 19 Mar 2026 Weekly Rate Dublin Permanent Wholetime Closing Date: 09 Apr 2026, 12:00 PM **Job Summary** --------------- EDUCATION, TRAINING, EXPERIENCE, ETC. Each candidate must, on the latest date for receipt of completed application form: Have a reasonable standard of education sufficient to enable them to perform the job efficiently and effectively – not less than junior certificate / intermediate certificate or equivalent. AND Hold a full unendorsed driving licence (Category B or equivalent) that permits permanent driving in Ireland AND Hold a valid work permit if required\* * All non\-EU/EEA citizens must hold a valid work permit prior to and for the duration of their contract. The work permit must allow you to work full time for Dún Laoghaire Rathdown County Council. It is the responsibility of individual employees to ensure that you have a valid work permit. If at any stage during your contract you cease to hold a valid work permit you must immediately advise Dún Laoghaire\-Rathdown County Council and your employment will cease with immediate effect. It is desirable for candidates to: * have a qualification in horticulture, landscaping, ecology or a related discipline and practical horticulture and practical arboriculture; * Hold a current Safe Pass Card; * Have an awareness of the Council’s Biodiversity and Climate Action Plans and Tree Policy **Contact Details** ------------------- hr@dlrcoco.ie
